volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 185 - Section 345)

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: τάχʼ ἐς δόμους σοὺς τὸν πολύζηλον πόσιν
sections.translation: That your much-admired husband will soon come to your house , revealed in his victorious might .

sections.label: Section 187
sections.original: καὶ τοῦ τόδʼ ἀστῶν ἢ ξένων μαθὼν λέγεις ;
sections.translation: From what citizen or what stranger did you learn these tidings ?

sections.label: Section 188
sections.original: ἐν βουθερεῖ λειμῶνι πρὸς πολλοὺς θροεῖ
sections.translation: In the meadow , the summer haunt of oxen , Lichas the herald is proclaiming it to many . From him I heard it ,

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: ἀπῇξʼ , ὅπως τοι πρῶτος ἀγγείλας τάδε
sections.translation: and dashed away so that , in all honesty , having been the first to report this news , I might profit in some way by you and win your favor .

sections.label: Section 192
sections.original: αὐτὸς δὲ πῶς ἄπεστιν , εἴπερ εὐτυχεῖ ;
sections.translation: And why is he not here , if he is so successful ?

sections.label: Section 193
sections.original: οὐκ εὐμαρείᾳ χρώμενος πολλῇ , γύναι .
sections.translation: He does not , my lady , enjoy ease of movement . The entire Malian populace

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: κρίνει παραστάς , οὐδʼ ἔχει βῆναι πρόσω ·
sections.translation: stands about him in a circle and questions him , and he cannot move forward . Each person is bent on learning according to his desire , and will not release the man until he has heard his pleasure . Thus against his wishes , but in keeping with theirs , he is with them . Yet you will soon see him face to face .

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , τὸν Οἴτης ἄτομον ὃς λειμῶνʼ ἔχεις ,
sections.translation: O Zeus , ruler of the sacred uncut meadow of Oeta , at last , though after much delay , you have given us joy ! Uplift your voices , you women within the house and you beyond our gates , since now we enjoy the brightness of this message , which has risen on us beyond my hope !

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: ἀνολολυξάτω δόμοις ἐφεστίοις
sections.translation: Let the brides of tomorrow raise a joyous cry for the house with shouts of triumph at the hearth . Among them let the yell of the men go up in unison for Apollo of the bright quiver , our defender ! And at the same time ,

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: παιᾶνα παιᾶνʼ ἀνάγετʼ , ὦ παρθένοι ,
sections.translation: maidens , lift up a paean , cry aloud to his sister , Ortygian Artemis , huntress of deer , goddess with torch in each hand ,

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: ἀείρομαι οὐδʼ ἀπώσομαι
sections.translation:
and to the nymphs her neighbors !
I am uplifted , I will not spurn the flute — O you master of my heart ! Behold , his ivy stirs me ! Euoe !

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: ὑποστρέφων ἅμιλλαν . ἰὼ ἰὼ Παιάν .
sections.translation: Quickly it wheels me round in Bacchus ’ s race ! Oh , oh , Paean ! Look , dear lady ! All is taking shape , plain to see , before your gaze .

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: ὁρῶ , φίλαι γυναῖκες , οὐδέ μʼ ὄμματος
sections.translation: I see it , dear maidens ; the sight has not escaped my watchful eyes . I see that procession . I bid the herald joyous welcome after his long absence !— if indeed you bring anything that gives joy .

sections.label: Section 229
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ μὲν ἵγμεθʼ , εὖ δὲ προσφωνούμεθα ,
sections.translation: We are happy in our return , and happy in your greeting ,

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: γύναι , κατʼ ἔργου κτῆσιν · ἄνδρα γὰρ καλῶς
sections.translation: lady , in accordance with the deed achieved . For when a man has fair fortune , it is his right to win good welcome .

sections.label: Section 232
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἀνδρῶν , πρῶθʼ ἃ πρῶτα βούλομαι
sections.translation: Most welcome man , tell me first what first I would know — shall I receive Heracles alive ?

sections.label: Section 234
sections.original: ἔγωγέ τοι σφʼ ἔλειπον ἰσχύοντά τε
sections.translation: I certainly left him alive and well ,

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: καὶ ζῶντα καὶ θάλλοντα κοὐ νόσῳ βαρύν .
sections.translation: in vigorous health , unburdened by disease .

sections.label: Section 236
sections.original: ποῦ γῆς ; πατρῴας εἴτε βαρβάρου ; λέγε .
sections.translation: Where , tell me — in his ancestral land , or on barbarian soil ?

sections.label: Section 237
sections.original: ἀκτή τις ἔστʼ Εὐβοιίς , ἔνθʼ ὁρίζεται
sections.translation: There is a headland of Euboea , where to Cenaean Zeus he marks out altars and fruitful ground in tribute .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.original: εὐκταῖα φαίνων ἢ ἀπὸ μαντείας τινός ;
sections.translation: In payment of a vow , or at the command of an oracle ?

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: εὐχαῖς ὅθʼ ᾕρει τῶνδʼ ἀνάστατον δορὶ
sections.translation: For a vow , made when he was seeking to conquer and plunder the country of these women whom you see before you .

sections.label: Section 242
sections.original: αὗται δέ , πρὸς θεῶν , τοῦ ποτʼ εἰσὶ καὶ τίνες ;
sections.translation: And these — who are they , by the gods , and whose daughters ? They deserve pity , unless their misfortune deceives me .

sections.label: Section 244
sections.original: ταύτας ἐκεῖνος Εὐρύτου πέρσας πόλιν
sections.translation: These are captives whom he

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ἐξείλεθʼ αὑτῷ κτῆμα καὶ θεοῖς κριτόν .
sections.translation: selected as choice spoils for himself and for the gods when he sacked the city of Eurytus .

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: ἦ κἀπὶ ταύτῃ τῇ πόλει τὸν ἄσκοπον
sections.translation: Was it in fact the war against that city which kept him away so long , beyond all forecast , past all count of days ?

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ τὸν μὲν πλεῖστον ἐν Λυδοῖς χρόνον
sections.translation: No . The greater part of the time he was detained in Lydia , no free man , as he declares ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐμποληθείς · τοῦ λόγου δʼ οὐ χρὴ φθόνον ,
sections.translation: but sold into servitude . No offense should be taken at my tale , lady , when the deed is found to be Zeus ’ work . He passed a whole year , as he himself says , a bought slave to the barbarian Omphale . And so stung was he by the shame of it ,

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ὥσθʼ ὅρκον αὑτῷ προσβαλὼν διώμοσεν ,
sections.translation: that he bound himself by a solemn oath , swearing one day to enslave with wife and child the man who had brought that suffering upon him . And not in vain did he speak the oath ; but , when he had been purified , he gathered a mercenary army and went against the city

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: τὴν Εὐρυτείαν . τόνδε γὰρ μεταίτιον
sections.translation: of Eurytus . For , Heracles asserted , that man alone of mortals had a share in causing his suffering . For when Heracles , a guest-friend of long standing , came to his house and hearth , Eurytus roared against him with insults of ruinous intent ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: λέγων χεροῖν μὲν ὡς ἄφυκτʼ ἔχων βέλη
sections.translation: saying that , although Heracles had inevitable shafts in his hands , he fell short of his own sons in the contest of the bow . Next he shouted that Heracles was a freeman ’ s slave , a broken hulk , and then at a banquet , when his guest was full of wine , he tossed him from his home . 270 Furious at this treatment ,

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: ὡς ἵκετʼ αὖθις Ἴφιτος Τιρυνθίαν
sections.translation: when afterward Iphitus came to the hill of Tiryns on the track of horses that had strayed , Heracles seized a moment when the man ’ s eyes were one place and his thoughts another , and hurled him from a towering summit . But in anger at that deed , the king ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ὁ τῶν ἁπάντων Ζεὺς πατὴρ Ὀλύμπιος
sections.translation: the father of all , Olympian Zeus , sent him away to be sold , and did not tolerate that this once , he killed a man by guile . Had he achieved his vengeance openly , Zeus would surely have pardoned him the righteous triumph .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: ὕβριν γὰρ οὐ στέργουσιν οὐδὲ δαίμονες .
sections.translation:
For the gods do not love criminal behavior either .
So those men , who gloried in bitter speech , are themselves residents of Hades , all of them , and their city is enslaved . And the women whom you see , fallen from happiness to misery ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: χωροῦσι πρὸς σέ · ταῦτα γὰρ πόσις τε σὸς
sections.translation: are sent here to you . For that was your husband ’ s command , which I , his faithful servant , perform . As for the man himself , know that he will come , once he has made pure sacrifice to Zeus of his fathers for the sacking of the city . After all the good news

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: πολλοῦ καλῶς λεχθέντος ἥδιστον κλύειν .
sections.translation: that has been told , this , indeed , is the sweetest word to hear .

sections.label: Section 291
sections.original: ἄνασσα , νῦν σοι τέρψις ἐμφανὴς κυρεῖ ,
sections.translation: Now , O Queen , your joy is plainly revealed ; part is with you , and of the rest you have foreknowledge .

sections.label: Section 293
sections.original: πῶς δʼ οὐκ ἐγὼ χαίροιμʼ ἄν , ἀνδρὸς εὐτυχῆ
sections.translation: Yes , how would I not rejoice with dutiful spirit , when I hear of my husband ’ s successful venture ?

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: πολλή ʼστʼ ἀνάγκη τῇδε τοῦτο συντρέχειν .
sections.translation: My rejoicing is bound to keep pace with his success . And yet a prudent mind can see room for fear , lest he who prospers should one day stumble . For a strange pity came over me , friends , at the sight of these ill-fated exiles ,

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: χώρας ἀοίκους ἀπάτοράς τʼ ἀλωμένας ,
sections.translation: homeless and fatherless in a foreign land . Though they were once the daughters perhaps of freeborn men , they now live the life of slaves . O Zeus , god who turns the tide of battle , may I never see you stalking against a child of my line on any occasion ;

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: μηδʼ , εἴ τι δράσεις , τῆσδέ γε ζώσης ἔτι .
sections.translation: no , if you do some such thing , let it not be while I am still alive ! So great is my fear , as I look upon these girls .

sections.label: Section 307
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλαινα , τίς ποτʼ εἶ νεανίδων ;
sections.translation: Unfortunate girl , who are you ? Are you without a man , or are you a mother ? To judge by your appearance , you are untried in those roles , but someone of noble birth .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: Λίχα , τίνος ποτʼ ἐστὶν ἡ ξένη βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: Lichas , whose daughter is this stranger ? Who is her mother , who the father who begot her ? Speak ; I pity her more than all the rest , when I look at her , inasmuch as she alone knows self-control .

sections.label: Section 314
sections.original: τί δʼ οἶδʼ ἐγώ , τί δʼ ἄν με καὶ κρίνοις ; ἴσως
sections.translation: Why would I know ? Why would you ask me ? Perhaps

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: γέννημα τῶν ἐκεῖθεν οὐκ ἐν ὑστάτοις .
sections.translation: she is the offspring of not the lowest family in that land .

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: μὴ τῶν τυράννων ; Εὐρύτου σπορά τις ἦν ;
sections.translation: Can she be of the ruling family ? Was she a child of Eurytus ?

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα · καὶ γὰρ οὐδʼ ἀνιστόρουν μακράν .
sections.translation: I do not know ; I did not in fact make a long investigation .

sections.label: Section 318
sections.original: οὐδʼ ὄνομα πρός του τῶν ξυνεμπόρων ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: And you do not know her name from one of her fellow captives ?

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: ἥκιστα · σιγῇ τοὐμὸν ἔργον ἤνυτον .
sections.translation: No , indeed . In silence I completed my task .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: εἴπʼ , ὦ τάλαινʼ , ἀλλʼ ἡμὶν ἐκ σαυτῆς , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: Unhappy girl , tell it to me , anyway , from your own mouth . It is indeed distressing to me not to know who you are .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: οὔ τἄρα τῷ γε πρόσθεν οὐδὲν ἐξ ἴσου
sections.translation: It will not , I assure you , be at all on a par with time past if she moves her lips . She has not said a word , large or small ,

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αἰὲν ὠδίνουσα συμφορᾶς βάρος
sections.translation: but has been constantly laboring with the heavy pains of her misfortune and weeping , poor girl , since she left her windswept fatherland . Her condition is indeed bad — for her , anyway — but claims our forbearance .

sections.label: Section 329
sections.original: ἡ δʼ οὖν ἐάσθω , καὶ πορευέσθω στέγας
sections.translation: Then let her be left in peace , and go beneath our roof

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: οὕτως ὅπως ἥδιστα , μηδὲ πρὸς κακοῖς
sections.translation: as it pleases her . Let her not in addition to her existing troubles take fresh grief from me ; she has enough already . Now let us all go in , so that you may go speedily on your journey , while I make all things ready in the house . Exit Lichas , followed by the Captives , into the house .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: αὐτοῦ γε πρῶτον βαιὸν ἀμμείνασʼ , ὅπως
sections.translation: Do go in , but first remain here a short while , so that you may learn , apart from these others , who they are whom you take into your home , and gain necessary knowledge of the facts which you have not heard . For of these I am in full possession .

sections.label: Section 339
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστί ; τοῦ με τήνδʼ ἐφίστασαι βάσιν ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Why do you stay my departure ?

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: σταθεῖσʼ ἄκουσον · καὶ γὰρ οὐδὲ τὸν πάρος
sections.translation: Stop and listen . My former report was not a waste of your time , and neither , I believe , will this one be .

sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: πότερον ἐκείνους δῆτα δεῦρʼ αὖθις πάλιν
sections.translation: Shall I call those others back again ? Or do you wish to speak before me and these women ?

sections.label: Section 344
sections.original: σοὶ ταῖσδέ τʼ οὐδὲν εἴργεται , τούτους δʼ ἔα .
sections.translation: To you and the women I can speak freely . Never mind the others .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: καὶ δὴ βεβᾶσι , χὠ λόγος σημαινέτω .
sections.translation: Well , they are gone . Now let your speech signal your meaning .
